def: s.f. lit. 'Triple-braid'; the place (now called Allahabad) where the Ganges unites with the Jamnā and is supposed to receive underground the Sarasvatī; the confluence of three sacred rivers.
more: n. f. lit . Three braids of hair. The confluence of three sacred rivers, especially that of the Ganges, Jamnā, and the mythical Saraswati under ground at Allahabad. 1. Sīs kes bin, chuṭyā tīn, augan let parāye chhīn, Jo jāë unke darbār, tāke mūṅḍ na rākhe bār . Riddle. Ans. Tribenī . A hairless head, yet topknots three, your sins it takes away, All who unto its courts repair bald-headed come away. 2. Gangā tirbenī, baikunṭh kī nasainī . Bhajan. The three-forked Ganges, the ladder to Heaven.
s. f. The conflux of three sacred rivers; especially, that of the Ganges, Jamunā, and supposed Saraswatī under ground at Ilāhābād, which city is also so named.

An aggregate of three things or substances, &c.; the three objects or pursuits of life, viz. religion or virtue (dharm), pleasure (kām), and wealth (arth); the three conditions of a king or of a state, viz. progress (vr̤iddhi), remaining stationary (sthāna), and decline (kshay), or gain, equality, and loss; the three qualities of nature, viz. purity, blindness, and depravity; the three higher castes or tribes.

def: s.m. The three steps or strides (of Vishṇu); one who makes three steps or strides, an epithet of Vishṇu (who paced the three worlds in three steps in his Vāman avatār or 'dwarf incarnation').

s.f. A woman, female; a wife
more: n. f. S. स्त्री A female; a woman. tiryā chillattar , n. m. Woman's wiles, craft, deep stratagem; the mysterious, unfathomable artfulness of women. 1. Tiryā chillattar jāne na koë, Khasam mār-ke sattī hoë . Prov. The deep deceit of woman sure no man can ever know, She kills her lord, then immolates herself her truth to show. 2. Kahā kusam kā oṛhnā, būṅd paṛe ḍhal jāë? Kahā birānī tīryā, bhor bhaë uṭh jāë? Dohā. What's in a garment red—one drop just and its color's gone? What's in a mistress strange—with peep of early dawn she's flown? tiryā-rāj , n. m. 1. The rule of women. 2. Wife rule; petticoat government. tiryā-haṭ , n. f. Insistance; persistence (of a woman).
subs: s.f. The science or knowledge of women
vulg. tiryā-ćillattar, s.m. Women's ways; woman's wiles or artfulness
triyā-rājya, s.m. Woman's rule; the kingdom of women, Amazon country; wife-rule, petticoat-government
s.f. Woman's persistency, female persistence, or importunity, &c.

more: 1. Thou; thee. 2. The cry of the cuckoo. tū tū, maiṅ maiṅ , n. f. lit . Thou thou, I I. Wrangling; quarrel; abuse ( gālī galauj ). tū tū, maiṅ maiṅ k., tū tū, yā tū tāṛāq k . v. n. To thee and thou; to abuse.
pron. Thou. (Inflect. تجهـ tujh or تجهي tujhe, Thee). ¶ توتو tū-tū, Sound of calling a dog.
subs: tū-tū karnā, tū-karnā, v.t. To thou and thee, to address rudely or disrespectfully; to wrangle, quarrel; to abuse
s.f. 'Thou thou, I I'; altercation, wrangling, squabbling; abusing
v.t. To wrangle, squabble; to abuse

def: s.m. Heap, mound; stack; a mud pillar, boundary pillar, land-mark (syn. ḍholā); a mark or butt (to shoot at)

The old man boastful. Nazīr. My mind incessant still doth dumb-bells turn at will! And mounds are by my arrow pierced by strength and skill.

def: s.m. Spit, spittle; spray, foam (as of the sea); a curse; — intj. Shame!; fie!; a curse (on)!; ugh!; foh!; faugh! (e.g. tuf hai uskī auqāt par; aise nikhaṭṭū marduʼe-kī rīsh-par tuf hai).
more: intj. Tush! pish! faugh! foh! fie! ugh! Buā, aise nikhaṭṭū mardūë kī rīsh par tuf hai! Nigoṛā māṅgtā jorū se hai paisā hajāmat kā . Jān Sāhab. 'Shame on the beard of such an earn-nothing, I say! 'My dear, he wants his wife his shaving-man to pay.'

def: (dim. of t̤ifl, q.v.), s.m. & f. A companion, a humble follower; a parasite; a glutton; one who uninvited accompanies one of the guests to a feast, one who sponges (in this sense said to be derived from Tufail, a poet of Kūfā, who was noted as a feast-hunter); cause, means, instrumentality, intervention (syn. wasīla; z̤arīʻa)
more: n. m. Intervention; mediation (ذريعه). tufail, tufail se , adv. Through the merits or agency of ( duāre ). Tumhāre tufail se aur das ādmi khā leṅge. tufailī , adj. Sponging; parasitical. tufail' yā , n. m. One who sponges (H. bargī ). Ham kyā tufaīliyoṅ meṅ haiṅ jo bagair bulāë dāvat meṅ jâëṅ?
s. m. f. A parasite, a glutton, one who uninvited accompanies one of the guests to a feast; (used adverbially) By the means, through, for the sake.
subs: (kisī-ke), By means (of), through the merits or agency (of), &c.

def: adj. Sponging, parasitical.
s.m. One who sponges, &c. ( = t̤ufail).

ety: v.n. fr. طوف 'to surround, go round, envelop,' &c.
def: s.m. A violent storm of wind and rain, a tempest, typhoon; a flood, deluge, inundation; the universal deluge; a flood or torrent (of obloquy, &c.); — a commotion, noise, riot; a gross wrong; calumny (syn. andher); a great trial, or affliction, or calamity; a mischievous imp; a firebrand
more: n. m. طوف went round. 1. A storm of wind and rain (اَندهي). Laṛkā kyā hai, ek tūfān hai . Prov. Saying. A child you call him, a whirlwind rather say. 2. An inundation; a deluge; the universal deluge. 3. Noise; riot. 4. Calumny ( bohtān ). 1. Tūfān shaitān, Allah nigah-bān! Wom. Prov. From the tempest of calumny, O Lord! deliver us! 2. Maiṅ ne kab kī thī bhalā kuchh aur ḍhab kī bāt-chīt, Qahr ṭūṭe gaib kā bohtān aur tūfān par! Wom. Insha. When, goodness me! have I e'er talked in prurient strain? Heaven's vengeance fall upon such slanderous tongues profane! 5. A fire brand ( agyāhā ). Laṛke! tū to ek tūfān hai! tūfān uṭhānā, yā machānā , v. a. To make a great noise. tūfān ānā , v. n. 1. To blow a hurricane. 2. To come on (a flood). tūfān khaṛā k., bāṅdhnā, joṛnā, lenā, yā lagānā , v. a. To calumniate; slander. 1. Kyoṅ murde par tūfān letī hai? Wom. Why do you slander the dead? 2. Nahā dho-…

(from طوف To go round, to encircle; or, rather, Ch. and Syr. from Ch. and To drop, to flow, to overflow) s. m. 1. An inundation or deluge. 2. The universal deluge. 3. A hurricane, a storm of wind and rain (analog. to Gr. Τυφων ). طوفان طرازي t̤ūfān-t̤arāz-ī, s. f. Deluge-making. 4. ( met. ) Calumny, defamation. طوفان لگانا t̤ūfān lagānā, or طوفان لينا t̤ūfān lenā, To calumniate, defame. 5. ( met. ) A quarrelsome person. Hidāyat says: اشک گلگون جيب ودامان پرمري غلطان هين اس زماليکي تو لڙکي بهـي کوٴي طوفان هين Ashk-i-gulgūṅ jaib o dāmāṅ par mire ghalt̤ān haiṅ; is zamāne ke to laṛke bhī koʼī t̤ūfān haiṅ, The tears of blood fall on the collar, nay even to the skirt of my garment; even the children of the present day are promoters of quarrel.
subs: v.n. To raise a commotion, make a great noise, &c.
or t̤ūfān lagānā, or t̤ūfān lenā (-par), To asperse, calumniate, &c. ( = t̤ūfān bāṅdhnā)
v.n. = t̤ūfān uṭhānā and t̤ūfān karnā.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian طوفان (tūfān), from Arabic طُوفَان (ṭūfān).

def: s.m. A tube through which a thing is blown or propelled; a musket; pea-shooter; pop-gun.
more: n. f. Any tube through which a thing is blown; a musket.
s. f. A musket (properly, any tube through which a thing is blown).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تفنگ (tufang), perhaps from Khorezmian Turkic, but more likely via Chagatai through the Mughal Empire whose rulers were initially Chagatai-speaking, and established one of the three "gunpowder empires" alongside the Ottoman Empire and Safavid Iran. The word is itself derived as an onomatopoeia mimicking the sound of blowpipes used to kill birds.

def: s.m. A musketeer, matchlock-man.

adj. & s.m. Stormy, tempestuous; of or relating to a deluge; (met.) boisterous, quarrelsome, violent, noisy, riotous; slanderous; — a boisterous fellow; a mischief-making person, an incendiary; a slanderer, calumniator.
more: adj. See daṅgai . 1. Boisterous; violent; riotous. 2. Slanderous. tūfānī ; Wom. tufānī , n. m. 1. An incendiary ( āg-lagāū ). 2. See tohmatī .
adj. 1. Stormy, tempestuous, of a deluge. 2. ( met. ) Quarrelsome, boisterous.

ety: for A. طفولية t̤ufūlīyat, fr. طفل
def: s.f. State or condition of an infant, or of a child; infancy; childhood.
s.f. The manna of bamboos ( = tabāshīr)

ety: said to be corr. fr. A. طغرة t̤ag̠rat, fr. طغر, a dial. var., or a corr. of دغر 'to thrust'; 'to press'; &c.; cf. A dag̠rā
def: s.m. A kind of handwriting, the large thick characters in which the royal titles prefixed to letters, diplomas, or other public deeds are generally written; (hence) the royal titles prefixed to letters, diplomas, &c.; the imperial signature.
more: s. m. 1. The royal titles prefixed to publick papers. 2. A sort of writing.

ety: inf. n. of طغو, or طغي 'To exceed just measure,' &c.
def: s.m. Disobedience, rebellion, sedition, perverseness, insolence.
more: (from طغي Heb. or To err, to stray, to rebel) s. m. Rebellion, sedition, perverseness, insolence.

ety: t̤ug̠yān, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Excess, exorbitance; overflowing, inundation, flood, deluge.
more: s. f. Excess, overflowing, deluge, inundation.

def: adj. (dialectic) = terā (see toh).
pron. Thou alone (God)! tuhī tuhī , The note of the smaller skulking warbler.
pron. (emphat. of tū), Thou, thyself; thou alone; — (in Braj, dat. & acc. of tū), to thee.
n. f. 1. The twee twee of the common warbler. 2. ( tū hī ) God alone.
s.m. Snow, frost, &c. = tuhin, q.v.
s.m. Mist, dew; hoar-frost, ice, snow; cold; moonlight, moonshine (cf. tushār)
s. m. Purification, cleanliness, purity.

ety: inf. n. of طہر; see t̤ahārat
def: s.m. Purification; cleanliness, purity.
v.n. = ṭhusaknā and ṭusaknā, q.v. v.n. To weep silently or softly; to sob ( = ṭusaknā); peditum ciere, suppedere.
adj. = ṭuṅʼiyā, q.v. adj. Very small, tiny (cf. ṭunj)
subs: s.m. A small species of parrot; a paroquet.

def: adj. Tiny; very small. ṭuīyāṅ totā , n. m. A kind of parrot.
pron. inflect. of tū . Saudā, Walī. (Obs). Thee; thine; of thee.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa तुज्झु (tujjhu), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀚𑁆𑀛𑀁 (tujjhaṃ), from Sanskrit तुभ्यम् (tubhyam).

def: pron. inflect. (of تو tū ) Thee; thine, of thee.
pron. inflect. sing. S. पुष्मद Pr. tujjhe you. Thee; to thee. tujh se; Pr. and W. Rus. tut te . From thee. tujh mujh, lit . you and me. Every body.
more: pron. inflect. sing. Thee, to thee.

def: s.m. Merchants; (in Urdū) a merchant (generally sing. in Urdū, unless it occur in Arabic phrases, as malikuʼt-tujjār).
more: (plur. of تاجر used as a singular in Hind.) s. m. A merchant.

def: s.m. (f. ?), One line of a poem; a rhyme; a cadence; — a moment (cf. ṭuk)
more: n. m. A rhyme. tuk joṛnā, tuk-bandī k. , v. n. To make verses (mostly rhyme without reason). tuk meṅ tuk milānā , v. n. 1. To rhyme. 2. To concert measures; to side with; say ditto. be-tukī , n. f. 1. Without rhyme or reason; irrelevant, unconnected. 2. Neither head nor tail ( sir nā pāoṅ ). Achchhī be-tukī uṛāī!
Rus., adv. S. स्तोक A while; for a while. Ṭuk jīë to kyā jīë! Prov. Too brief a life of joy! who'd care such life to enjoy! ṭuk-sā, ṭuk-ek , adj. A little; very little ( zarā sā ).
s. f. 1. One line of a poem, a rhyme. 2. A moment.
subs: tuk joṛnā, v.n. To rhyme; to make verses
v.n. To rhyme; to concert (with); to sing the same song (as), to agree (with), side (with).

ety: Prk. ठोक्कं; S. स्तोकं, cf. ṭūk
def: adj. & adv. A little; a little while
subs: ṭuk-ek, adj. & s.m. A little, very little; — a little bit; a little way.

def: s.m. A small piece, a bit, a little, a particle, an atom (see ṭukṛā)
more: s. m. 1. A piece, a little, a particle, an atom, a bit. 2. A single beat of a drum.
subs: v.t. To break to pieces, &c. = ṭukṛā-ṭukṛā karnā, q.v.s.v. ṭukṛā
adj. & s.m. A little; — a small piece ( = thoṛā-sā)
s.m. An outspoken answer, the plain truth, a decisive answer, a flat refusal
v.t. To break or divide in two; to settle, decide.

def: s.m. Fear of God, piety ( = taqwā; taqīya).
s.m. A small piece, &c. = ṭūk, q.v.; — a single beat of a drum. s.m. (f. ?), One line of a poem; a rhyme; a cadence; — a moment (cf. ṭuk)
more: s. m. 1. A piece, a little, a particle, an atom, a bit. 2. A single beat of a drum.
subs: tuk joṛnā, v.n. To rhyme; to make verses
v.n. To rhyme; to concert (with); to sing the same song (as), to agree (with), side (with).

def: s.f. = tugā-kshīrī, q.v.s.v. tugā.
s.m. A piece, bit, morsel, scrap, &c.; a part, portion, division, fraction, section, paragraph, clause; a bit of bread; subsistence, livelihood
subs: s.m. One who lives on or who devours scraps or leavings; one who sponges; a dependant
s.m. One who begs for scraps or morsels of food, a beggar
(-ko), To give a scrap or morsel of bread or food (to); to allot in small shares, to dole out
(-ko), To break, tear, pull, or cut to pieces; to hack, chop up, make mince-meat (of); to cut to pieces; to analyze
(-ko), To break or divide into pieces; to divide, apportion, parcel out
shafaq-kā ṭukrā, s.m. A beautiful or lovely creature.

def: = H ٿکرا टुकरा ṭukrā, See ٿکڙ ṭukaṛ and ٿکڙا ṭukṛā.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀝𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀭 (ṭakkara). Cognate with Punjabi ਟੱਕਰ (ṭakkar) / ٹَکَّر (ṭakkar), Gujarati ટક્કર (ṭakkar), Marathi टक्कर (ṭakkar), Bengali টক্কর (ṭokkor).

def: v.t. See s.v. tū. pron. Thou (used to imply depreciation or contempt; or by way of familiarity, or endearment; or, in addressing the Deity, to imply extreme reverence)
more: v. a. To thou. (From تو tū. French, tutoyer).
subs: tū-tū karnā, tū-karnā, v.t. To thou and thee, to address rudely or disrespectfully; to wrangle, quarrel; to abuse
s.f. 'Thou thou, I I'; altercation, wrangling, squabbling; abusing
v.t. To wrangle, squabble; to abuse

def: s.m. = tushār, q.v. adj. & s.m. Cold, frigid, frosty; dewy; — cold, frost, ice, snow, hail; mist, dew, thin rain, fog, blight; — a crop that ripens in the cold season
more: s. m. Ice, snow, or frost. See تسار tusār.
subs: s.m. The snowy mountains, the Himālaya.

def: part. adj. = tushit, q.v. s.m. A class of subordinate deities (thirty-six in number); one of this class of deities.

ety: Pehl. tokhm; Zend taokhman, rt. tuć; S. tokman
def: s.m. Seed; sperm; an egg; a testicle; origin, principle
more: s. m. 1. Seed. 2. An egg. 3. Sperm. 4. A testicle. تخم بالنگو tuḵẖm-i-bālangū, s. m. A seed of a cooling quality. تخم بد tuḵẖm-i-bad, adj. Low-bred. تخم ريحان tuḵẖm-i-rīḥān, s. m. The seed of Ocymum pilosum: see ببي babai. تخم ريزي tuḵẖm-rezī, s. f. Scattering seed, sowing. تخم کاهو tuḵẖm-i-kāhū, s. m. Seed of lettuce. (Lactuca sativa). تخم کتان tuḵẖm-i-katān, s. m. Linseed.
subs: s.m. The seed of sweet basil, or of the mountain balm; a seed of cooling quality
adj. 'Bad seed'; of bad stock, low-bred
tuḵẖm-rezī, s.f. Scattering seed, sowing; tuḵẖm-pāshī karnā, v.n. To sow seed
s.f. Purslain; the seed of Ocymum pilosum; a cooling drink
s.m. Seed of lettuce, Lactuca sativa
wety: From Classical Persian تخم (tuxm, “seed; semen; origin”).

ety: for A. تخمة, from تخم for وخم
def: s.m. Indigestion, dyspepsia.
wety: From Classical Persian تخمه (tuxma, “descent; extraction, stock”).

def: s.m. See تكه tukka.
more: s. m. 1. A sort of arrow blunt at the end. 2. A small hill, an eminence. See تکه tukka.

def: s.m. An arrow without a head, but with a button or knob at the end; — a little hill, an eminence.
more: n. m. An arrow without a head. 1. Lagā to tīr, nahīṅ tukka . Prov. saying. No better than a straw a whit the dart that fails its mark to hit. 2. Khaṛī kamar tukkā sī . Boy's game. No stooping now, you must stand as straight as an arrow.

def: s.f. = H تکلا तुकला tuklā, s.m. A small paper kite (syn.guḍḍī).
more: n. f. A kind of paper kite.
s. f. A kind of paper kite.
s. A kind of paper kite (raised by means of a thin string, and resembling, except in shape, the European kite: Qanoon-e-islam ).

ety: corr. of tarkash; but cf. tukka
def: s.m. A quiver.
more: (corr. of ترکش) s. m. A quiver.

def: s.m. = H تکل तुक्कल tukkal, s.f. A small paper kite (syn.guḍḍī).
more: s. m. A kind of paper kite.

def: s.f. A child's paper kite.
more: (dimin. of تکلا) s. f. A (childʼs) kite.

ety: prob. connected with tukka, and S. twak
def: s.m. (Orig. 'a button'), a loop, button-loop, button-hole, eye-loop.
more: n. m. Loop; eyeloop; button hole.
s. m. A button, a loop, an eyeloop. تکمه جيب کهولنا tukma-i-jaib kholnā, To open the purse.

def: v. a. (dakh.) To regard with a fixed look, to gaze.
A kind of small basket (used for grain, &c.).
s.m. A piece, bit, morsel, scrap, &c.; a part, portion, division, fraction, section, paragraph, clause; a bit of bread; subsistence, livelihood
more: s. m. A piece, a bit, a portion, division, &c. see ٿکرا.
subs: s.m. One who lives on or who devours scraps or leavings; one who sponges; a dependant
s.m. One who begs for scraps or morsels of food, a beggar
(-ko), To give a scrap or morsel of bread or food (to); to allot in small shares, to dole out
(-ko), To break, tear, pull, or cut to pieces; to hack, chop up, make mince-meat (of); to cut to pieces; to analyze

shafaq-kā ṭukrā, s.m. A beautiful or lovely creature.
wety: Inherited from Proto-New Indo-Aryan *𑀝𑀼𑀓𑁆𑀓 (*ṭukka, “piece”) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀟- (-ḍa-) + -𑀓- (-ka-), from *टुक्क् (ṭukk, “to cut, break”), from Proto-Middle Indo-Aryan *𑀢𑁆𑀭𑀼𑀓𑁆𑀓𑁆 (*trukk). Cognate with Assamese টুকুৰা (tukura), Bengali টুকরা (ṭukra).

def: = H ٿکر टुकर ṭukar, See ٿکڙ ṭukaṛ and ٿکڙا ṭukṛā.
more: s. m. A piece, a division, a bit, a morsel. ٿکڙاپرزه ṭukṛā purza, s. m. Scraps, clippings, shavings. ٿکرگدا ṭukr-gadā or ٿکڙگدا ṭukṛ-gadā, A beggar for a bit of bread, &c.

def: s.f. See ṭukṛī.
s.f. A small piece, &c.; a small pane of glass; a piece of cloth.
more: s. f. A piece, a bit, a portion, division, &c. see ٿکرا.
s. f. (dakh.) Party: see جتها jathā.

ety: S. स्तवक+र+इका; but cf. ṭukṛī above
def: s.f. A party, band, troop; a division or corps (of an army); a flock (of birds).
s. f. Party; body, division: see ٿکڙي ṭukṛī.
v. a. Caus. of ṭoknā , q. v.
v.t. To cause to be challenged, to have (one) interrogated; to have (one) taken to task, &c. (see ṭoknā).

ety: Prk. तुल्ली, and तुल्लं; S. तुल्यः and तुल्यं
def: adj. & adv. Equal; like, similar; alike
more: adj. S. तुल्य Alike; like; as broad as it is long ( barābar ). tull-ārthak . Synonymous. tull-bhāg . Equal shares.

adj. Alike, like. تل بيٿهنا tul baiṭhnā, 1. To be weighed against valuable things, which are to be given in alms. 2. To sit straight and compact (as in a boat which might be overset by sitting uneven or carelessly). تل رهنا tul rahnā, To stand front to front, ready for battle or opposed.
subs: v.n. To turn out or prove to be equal; to be weighed against valuable things which are to be given in alms; to sit straight and evenly balanced (on a horse or in a boat; — tul, here, is probably the past conj. part. of tulnā)
(-ke), To stand facing or opposed (to), to confront, be drawn up in battle array.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit तिल (tila), from Sanskrit तिल (tila), borrowed from a Munda language.

def: s.m. = tul, and tulya, q.v. adj. & adv. Equal; like, similar; alike
subs: v.n. To turn out or prove to be equal; to be weighed against valuable things which are to be given in alms; to sit straight and evenly balanced (on a horse or in a boat; — tul, here, is probably the past conj. part. of tulnā)
(-ke), To stand facing or opposed (to), to confront, be drawn up in battle array.

def: s.m. Twill, twilled cloth.

ety: inf. n. of طول 'to be or become long,' &c.
def: s.m. Length; longitude; prolixity; diffuseness; — adj. Long, tall; extended, extensive, prolonged, lasting long
more: 1. s. m. Length, longitude; prolixity, diffusiveness. طول حرف t̤ūl-i-ḥarf, or طول سخحن t̤ūl-i-suḵẖan, or طول کلام t̤ūl-i-kalām, Prolixity, length of discourse, circumlocution, many words. 2. adj. Long, tall, extensive, lasting long.
subs: v.n. To assume length, to be drawn out, to be or become prolonged; to extend far or a long way
(-ko), To lengthen, to elongate, to make prolix, to spin a long yarn or rigmarole
or t̤ūl-ě-kālam, s.m. Length of discourse, many words, prolixity, circumlocution, verbosity

def: n. m. Length; longitude ( darāzī ). tūl-i-balad ; H. khaṛā chakr , n. m. S. दशांतर Longitude. tūl pakaṛnā yā kheṅchnā , v. n. To be prolonged (a matter); to go far. Yeh bīmārī tūl pakaṛ gaī tūl denā , v. a. To lengthen out; elongate ( baṛhānā 3). tūl kalām . Prolixity; circumlocution; verbosity; verbiage.
s.f. 'Cotton-tube'; a thick roll of cotton which is drawn out in spinning.
more: n. m. Cor. of Twilled cloth; twill. Aṅgyā na lāyā ṭūl kī! terī jal jāë lambardārī! Ballad. Relo. No boddice twilled you've brought for me! Fire burn your rent-paying dignity!
s. m. Weight, weighing. تول تال taul-tāl, Weighing and measuring.

def: s.f. A balance or scale, a pair of scales; the balance as an ordeal; the sign Libra of the Zodiac; a measure or weight of gold and silver, 100 palas, or about 145 oz. Troy; the practice of being weighed against gold or any valuable substance (which is afterwards given to priests); the science of Statics
more: n. m. S. तुला. 1. A balance or scale; a pair of scales. 2. Statics. 3. ( Ast .) Libra. tulā parīk'shā . Ordeal by the balance. The accused is first weighed in a pair of scales, then removed and, after certain ceremonies, he is again weighed. If he weighs lighter he is accounted innocent; if the same or heavier, he is guilty. tulā dān , n. m. A gift to Brahmans of gold, silver, rice, or other valuable article equal in weight to the person of the donor who is weighed against the article.
adj. Balanced; even; poised; equipoised; equalized. tulā rahnā , v. n. To sit straight in the middle (in a boat, on a horse, etc). an-tulā , adj. Not balanced. See atul .
s. f. 1. A balance or scale. 2. The sign Libra of the zodiack. 3. The practice of being weighed against gold or any kind of valuable substance, which is afterwards given to priests.
subs: s.f. Ordeal by the balance (the weight of the accused being first taken, certain prayers and ceremonies are performed, after which he is weighed again; if lighter, he is innocent; if heavier, or as at first, guilty)
s.m. The gift to a Brāhman of as much gold, silver, &c. as equals the weight of the body
s.f. An ornament of the feet or toes; a hundred millions
s.f. The entrance of the sun into Libra

or tulā-bīj, s.m. The berry of the Abrus precatorius, from which the goldsmith's or jeweller's weight in India is taken (the berry weighs about 1 5⁄16 grains Troy, the factitious weight about 2 3⁄16 grains).
wety: From Sanskrit.

def: part. adj. Weighed; poised, evenly balanced, in equipoise, in equilibrium; — tulā huʼā, adj. Idem
subs: v.n. To sit evenly balanced (in a boat, &c.), to sit straight or erect (on a horse; cf. tul baithnā); to be in equilibrium
s.m. A system of forces in equilibrium.
wety: From Sanskrit.

def: (prop. pl. of t̤alīb; but com. used in India for t̤alaba, pl. of t̤ālib), s.m. pl. 'Seekers after knowledge,' students.
s.f. = tulwāʼī, q.v. s.f. The act o weighing; price paid for having (a thing) weighed, weighment fee.
v. n. 1. To drop (a fruit from a creeper). 2. To move with feeble steps; to creep. 3. To die ( marnā ).
v.n. (dialec.) To drop (as fruit, &c.); to move with tottering or feeble steps, to totter.
Rus. n. f. Dripping; dropping; spout; stream.
adv. Lengthwise; in length.

ety: caus. of tulnā; tulā˚ = Prk. तल्लावे(इ) = S. तुल्य+आपि; cf. talānā
def: v.t. To cause to be weighed, to have or get (a thing) weighed, valued, &c. ( = taulānā, tulwānā).
adj. Lengthy; very tall; prolix; — (in Pers. & Hind.) s.f. Great length; prolixity.
s.f. The shrub holy basil, Ocymum sanctum (said to have been produced from the hair of the goddess Tulasī, and held in great veneration by Hindūs)
more: n. f. Sweet basil; Ocymum sacrum . Wat; a small shrub held in veneration by the Hindūs. Tulsi was a nymph beloved by Krishna and by him metamorphosed into this plant. tulsī phūl , E. n. m. A superior bāsmatī rice, so called from its smelling like tulsī . It is usually kept for rare occasions, as weddings, entertainments, etc. tulsī-dal , n. m. A leaf of the tulsī plant.

s. f. A small shrub held in veneration by the Hindūs, holy basil (Ocymum sanctum). Tulsī or Tulasī was a nymph beloved by Kr̤ish̤ṇa and by him metamorphosed into this plant. تلسي داس tulsī-dās, n. p. Name of a Gosāʼīṅ. تلسي دانه tulsī-dāna, A gold ornament. تلسي کاهيرا tulsī kā hīra, Beads made of the wood of the tulsī plant.
s. f. (dakh.) A necklace, nearly the same as the دلڙي du-laṛ-ī, q. v. except that the gold beads are of an octagonal shape ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
subs: s.m. Name of a superior kind of fragraṇt rice (so called from its smelling like tulsī); — tulsī-dāna, s.m. A gold ornament
s.m. The leaf of the tulsī plant; — tulsī-dal ćaṛhānā (-par), To place or sprinkle tulsī leaves on the head of an idol or of a Brāhman
s.m. A bead made of the wood of the tulsī.

ety: tulā, rt. of tulā-nā+Prk. इउओ = S. (इ)तृ+कः
def: adj. Balancing, producing equilibrium, statical.

ety: tulā˚, base of tulānā+āwā = Prk. एअव्वअं = S. (इ)तव्य+कं
def: s.m. The part of a (Hindūstānī) carriage which rests on the axle-tree arm and supports the body of the carriage.
more: n. m. H. tulnā , to be weighed. The part of a Native carriage which rests on the axle-tree and supports the body of the carriage. Tulāve par zor na do, ṭūṭ jāëgā .
(corrupt. for طليعه and طلايه) An advanced guard, forlorn hope, a patrole or reconnoitring party.
s. m. Part of a Hindūstanī carriage, which rests on the axletree arm and supports the body of the carriage.

def: s. (dakh.) See ٿهٿها ṭhaṭṭhā.

s.f. A fibrous stick used as a brush by weavers to clean the threads of the woof (or used as a brush generally, cf. tur); a painter's brush or pencil; a rod, &c., dipped into a crucible to ascertain whether the contents are in fusion.
more: n. f. A fibrous stick or brush used by weavers for cleaning the threads of the woof.

def: s.f. = tulī; tulā, qq.v. s.f. A fibrous stick used as a brush by weavers to clean the threads of the woof (or used as a brush generally, cf. tur); a painter's brush or pencil; a rod, &c., dipped into a crucible to ascertain whether the contents are in fusion.
more: n. f. 1. A society; a group (تَکرَي 3). 2. A band of musicians. 3. ( ṭolī ) A heavy stone.

def: s.f. Cotton; the wick of a lamp; a weaver's fibrous stick or brush; a painter's brush or pencil, or a stick with a fibrous extremity used as one; &c. ( = tulī, q.v.).
adj. Of or relating to length; long; tall.
s.f. A small balance, a steelyard.
s.m. Fox-grape; night-shade, Solanum nigrum.
more: s. m. Foxʼs grapes, nightshade. (Solanum nigrum).

def: s.f. A small bird (said to resemble the wag-tail).
s.f. The silk-cotton tree or Sīmal.

ety: tul˚ = Prk. तुल्ल(इ) = S. तुल्य(ते), pass. of rt. तुल्;+nā = Prk. अणअ = S. इनीयं
def: v.n. To be weighed, be balanced; to be suspended; to be drawn up in array (as one army against another); to lower; — v.t. To weigh; to value, appraise, &c. (See tolnā).
more: v. n. S. तुल् to weigh. 1. To be weighed or balanced. 2. To be drawn up in battle array. 3. To hang over; to be suspended; to lower. Kālī ghaṭāëṅ ā-kar ho mast tul rahī haiṅ . Nazīr. The black clouds gathering drunken reeled and hung. tulā rahnā . ( Mech .) To be in equilibrium. tulāū zor , ( Mech .) Statical force. tule mile zor . ( Mech .) A system of forces in equilibrium. sone chāṅdī meṅ tulnā, yā ladnā , v. n. To be loaded with jewels.
v. n. To be weighed or balanced; to be drawn up in array (as one army against another). See under تل tul.

def: v.n. To become soft (with moisture, as a mud wall, or a suppurating boil, &c.), to become pulpy, mellow, or ripe (as fruit); to spout or gush out; to drip.
more: v. n. 1. To become soft from moisture, as a mud wall in the rains, a suppurating sore, or ripening fruit. 2. To drip; pour in a stream; spout; gush.
v. n. To become soft (from moisture, as a mud wall in the rains, as a sore or bile when suppurating, or as a fruit getting ripe).

ety: inf. n. of طلع 'to rise' (as the sun, &c.
def: s.m. Rising, appearing (of the sun, &c.)
more: s. m. Rising (as, of the sun, &c.). طلوع هونا t̤ulūﻌ honā, To rise (the sun).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian طلوع (tulū'), from Arabic طُلُوع (ṭulūʕ).

def: s.f. The act o weighing; price paid for having (a thing) weighed, weighment fee.

ety: fr. tulwā-nā; aʼiyā = Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S.(इ)तव्य+क+इकः
def: s.m. A weigher, weighman.

ety: doub. cause of tolnā; — tulwā˚ = tul+āw redupl. = Prk. आवे = S. आपि
def: v.t. To have (a thing) weighed, &c. = tulānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to be weighed, to have or get (a thing) weighed, valued, &c. ( = taulānā, tulwānā).
more: v. a. Caus. of تولنا q. v. tulvāī, tulāī , n. f. Weighment fee.
v. n. (dakh.) To have the feet blistered from the heat of the sun.

def: s.m. Of equal strength
s.f. Equality; likeness, sameness, resemblance, analogousness.
more: E. n. f. 1. A young hen. 2. A fat young woman. ' Māmūṅ re, mamānī kaisī?' 'Chokar khā-ke dhokar aisī, Sir muṅḍā-ke ṭilyā aisī, Geṅd khā-ke paṭrā aisī .' E. Boys' play. 'How, uncle, flourishes my aunt'? 'Bran-fed, of fatness there's no want, Shaven her head, grown fat and plump, As ball 'gainst hard wood hits her—bump.'

def: the sun rose. Rising of the sun, etc. ( udaë , 1). tulū h. , v. n. To rise (the sun, etc).
pers. pron., 2nd pers. pl., You
more: pron. S. त्वम् Pr. tum, tumam thou. You; you all. Roṭī, kapṛā, us bhaṛve se āchhī ḍhāl likhāūṅgī, Nā likkhā tau tam donan ne ū ṭhāṅ kaid karāuṅgī . Wom. Rus. song. A separate maintenance bond this wh—son shall give me, or know, To prison under sentence you shall with my co-wife go.
2d pers. pron. plur. You. تم آپ tum āp, You yourself. تمکو tum ko or تمـﮩون کو tumhoṅ ko, To you.
subs: an inflective base, in Braj, of the dat., acc., and abl. cases of tum; — tum-ni-soṅ = tum-se; — tum-ni-kauṅ = tum-ko.
ex: کیا تم مجھ سے محبت کرتی ہو؟
kyā tum mujh-se mohabbat kartī ho?
تم جیسے لوگوں کی ہی ضرورت ہے یہاں۔
tum jaise logõ kī hī zarūrat hai yahā̃.
People like you are needed here.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa तुम्हि (tumhi), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂 (tumhe), from Sanskrit युष्मान् (yuṣmān, acc. pl.) adopting the initial sounds of त्वम् (tvám, nom. sg.).

def: s.f. (m.?) A trinket, ornament, jewels; trifles; any new and pretty thing, anything curious and striking; — a fine woman, a belle; — intestines, offal; — a frame for pigeons to perch on
more: s. m. 1. Trifles. 2. An ornament. 3. A frame for pigeons to perch on. ٿوم ٿام ṭūm-ṭām, s. m. 1. A few trifles. 2. An ornament. ٿوم دينا ṭūm denā, To push gently.

def: n. f. 1. A jewel; gold or silver ornament ( ṭūm chhallā ). 1. Jib tero hovegā chālā, dūṅgī ṭūm ghaṛā nānhā . Rus. Wom. song. When you leave for your new home in bridal array, I'll give you rich jewels you there shall display. 2. Joban bītā jāë, mātā, kar chāle kī tiārī, Jo tere ghar meṅ āsā nāhīṅ, ṭūm tū rakh de sārī Chaubolā . Mother, my rounded youth is fading, do my marriage speed, If you've no money in the house, your jewels pawn, L plead. 2. A handsome woman; a belle. Ghar meṅ woh zor raqam, turfa ṭūm thī . Fasānaë Ajāeb. 3. A sharp man. 4. Intestines; offal.

ety: from tumānā; āʼī = Prk. एअव्वअइआ = S. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका
def: s.f. Price paid for carding cotton.
s. f. The price of carding.

r: tuman, tǒman, lit, tuman bāṅdhnā, kā, tuman-dār
def: s.m. The commander of a tuman
more: s. m. 1. Brotherhood, connexion, cast, &c. 2. Bevy, crowd, troop, squadron (properly, ten thousand). See تومان tomān. تمن باندهنا tuman bāndhnā, To collect a body of troops. تمن دار tuman-dār, The commander of a tuman. تمنداري tuman-dār-ī, s. f. The command of a tuman.
subs: s.f. The command of a tuman.

def: v.t. To cause to be carded, to have or get (cotton) carded or cleaned.
more: (caus. of تومنا) v. a. To cause to be carded.

ety: v.n. fr. طمر, of which Form II. = ʻto fold or roll up'; — prob. akin to Gr. τόμος
def: s.m. 'A paper, &c. folded or rolled up,' a roll, scroll; a volume, book; an account-book; (met.) a long story; a heap
more: n. m. طمر heaped. 1. A heap ( aṭālā ). 2. A roll; volume. 3. A long or tedious story. tūmār bāṅdhnā , v. a. To go to great lengths; to make a long affair or story.
s. m. A book, a volume, a roll, an account-book, a story. جمع طومار jamʼiﻌ-t̤ūmār., The produce or collection of a land recorded in government books. طومار نويس t̤ūmār-navīs, An accountant.
subs: (-kā), To make a long story (of); to make much (of little); to go to great lengths
The produce or collection of a land recorded in Government books.

def: pron. (dakh.) Your: see تمـﮩ‍ارا tumhārā.
s.m. The gourd Lagenaria vulgaris; a hollowed gourd (in which mendicants carry water, &c.; see toṅbā, tomṛī, tūmṛī, &c.).
s.m. Wide drawers (almost like petticoats); drawers, short breeches.
more: n. m. Very wide drawers, almost like petticoats.

def: s.f. A long white gourd, Lagenaria vulgaris; a small hollow gourd (in which medicants carry water, &c.; cf. tumbā); a kind of pipe or musical instrument (made of the gourd) played on by snake-charmers, jugglers, &c. ( = tumṛī; tāṅbī, toṅbī).
pron. plur. See تمـﮩين tum-heṅ.
An optional base of the oblique cases of the pron. tum (but not commonly used, except in the dat.-acc. tumh-eṅ, the form tum being preferred).
The gen. masc. of the pron. tum (f. -ī), Of you; your, yours.
wety: Probably from Apabhramsa तुम्हारा (tumhārā), formed from Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂 (tumhe) or Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂𑀳𑀺𑀁 (tumhehiṃ) + را (rā, genitive affix) by analogy with 𑀅𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂 (amhe) or 𑀅𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂𑀳𑀺𑀁 (amhehiṃ) to अम्हारा (amhārā) or possibly inherited from Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑁆𑀳𑀸𑀡𑀁 (tumhāṇaṃ), genitive of 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂 (tumhe).

def: pron. m. tumhārī , f. S. युष्मद् Pr. tumo, tumha, tumma . Yours; of you; of thee. tumhārā kyā poṭā hai, tumhārī kyā bisāt hai, tum kyā kar sakte ho, tum kyā ho, tum ho kis lāyaq? Phrases expressing defiance or contempt of opposition.
pron. 2. pers. mas. genit. plur. Yourʼs, your.
pron. S. युष्मद्, , Pr. tumhe . Ye.
pron. plur. object. case, To you, you.
pron. plur. nomin. (dakh.) You.
s.m. Thread made of cotton that has been separated and loosened by the hand and then carded (as opp. to the coarser kind of thread made of akṛā rūʼī, or cotton that has been beaten only).
more: n. m. Thread made of cotton first picked with the fingers and then carded. Thread so treated is softer and finer, and used to be sold for its weight in silver.
s. m. Thread made of carded cotton, in opposition to that made of what has been beat only (called Ākrā rūʼī).

ety: tūm˚ = Prk. तुम्म(इ) = S. तुम्बय(ति), rt. तुम्ब
def: v.t. To pull (cotton or wool) to pieces and separate it with the fingers preparatory to combing
more: Wom. v. a. 1. To card or separate wool or cotton with the fingers, preparatory to combing. Age! baiṭhal kā kare hea? Ruiyā tūm ghāl to bhallo! Mag. I say! what's the good of your sitting there? if you picked the cotton it would be something. 2. To call names; to abuse roundly. Dekhyo kaisī dhūm ḍālūṅgī, Rūī ki tarah tūm ḍālūṅgī! Wom. Shauq.
v. a. To push or jog gently; to nudge.
v. a. To card or separate (wool or cotton) with the finger, preparatory to combing.
subs: v.t. intens. of and = tūmnā; (fig.) to pull (one) to pieces; to abuse roundly.

def: v.t. To push gently, to jog, nudge
v. a. (dakh.) See چهيڙنا chheṛnā.

ety: S. तुम्बि+ṛī = Prk. अडिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. A hollow gourd, &c. ( = tumbī; tūmbṛī, tombṛī).
s.f. = tūmṛī, q.v. s.f. A hollow gourd, &c. ( = tumbī; tūmbṛī, tombṛī).
more: n. f. S. तुम्बी 1. A hollowed gourd or calabash used by faqīrs , and swimmers. 2. A lantern made of watermelon hollowed within, or of earth, etc. Gar hai faqīr tū, to na rakh yāṅ kisī se mel, Yāṅ tomṛī na bel, paṛā sir pe apne khel . Nazīr. If thou 'rt in truth a devotee, all links with earth be far from thee, No need for gourd cup nor wild fruit, thy head the sport of nature free. 3. A firework consisting of a hollow earthen pot filled with powder.
(from तुम्बी see تومڙي) s. f. The snout of a crocodile, alligator, &c.

def: pron. See s.v. tum. pers. pron., 2nd pers. pl., You
more: pron. 2d pers. mas. gen. plur. Your, of you.

more: (vulg. t̤umtaṛāḳ ) s. m. Magnificence, grandeur, pomp.

The Toon tree, Cedrela toona (the wood of which bears some resemblance to mahogany, and is much used for furniture, &c.); — s.f. The flower of the Toon tree (used for dyeing a yellow colour)
s.m. A quiver.
s.m. Ventris crepitus
more: s. m. Ventris crepitus. ٿون کرنا ṭūṅ karnā, Crepitum ventris edere.

def: n. m. Ventris crepitus. "Ṭūṅ." "Sau kām chhoṛūṅ, terī khabar lūṅ!" Mazāq. ṭūṅ karnā , v. a. Crepitus ventris edere .
To dispute, to wrangle, to abuse.
v.n. To abort, to miscarry, to slip
more: E. v. n. ṭūṭnā to break (?). To abort. Bhains tū gaī. Tūī to mūī, nahīṅ tūī tau mūī . Prov. It's death to the mother whether her young dies in the womb, or she aborts.

n. m. 1. See افسون 1. Ādhī pūnī kachchā sūt, Maiṅ bāṅdhūṅ sāsū kā pūt, Bāṅdh-būṅdh-kar karūṅ gulām, Dehlī baiṭhā kare salām. Aisā ṭonā kar de, rī mā! Marriage song. Ṭonā . My mother, such a charm give me, That with raw thread but half a skein My spouse-to-be I might enchain. Twist, twist, and bind him fast till he My slave became and bowed to me! 2. Ṭonā lāgā mujhe, merā bāp lāgā tujhe . Marriage jest. (An abuse). 3. Sāṅvrā salonā, terī bāṅkī aṅkhyan meṅ ṭonā . Song. Ṭhumrī. Luxurious rich brunette, your wanton sportive glances have a spell for me. 2. A marriage song. Sahar kā woh honā, woh ṭone kā waqt, Woh dulhan kī rukhsat, woh rone kā waqt . Mīr. Hasan. ṭone-bāz , Men; ṭonhāī , Wom. n. One who enchants or casts spells. ṭonhā'ī, ṭonāhāī ; E. ṭonhā'in , n. f. Wom. 1. See ṭone-bāz . 2. One who is supposed to cure diseases by some superstitious charm. More bachchā kā ṭonhāin lihi sī, yeh…

def: adj. Little, small; thin, slight, slender; weak, delicate; effeminate; — s.m. A thin cake or fritter ( = tunkī)
more: adj. E. Weak; brittle; slight; thin. tunak-havās , adj. Easily affected; sensitive. tunak-havāsī , n. f. Sensibility; sensitiveness. tunak-zarf , adj. lit . A weak vessel. 1. Shallow; empty; unable to keep a secret ( ochhā ). 2. Easily upset by drink. tunak-mizāj , adj. Irascible; irritable; touchy; testy; pettish.
